label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the passage carefully to figure out why Babbage couldn't continue his research in 1828. The passage says: "When he returned to London in 1828, he was at the end of his resources. Many bills remained unpaid. His chief assistant and co-workers quarreled with him and left with many expensive tools. For one year no work was done." So the main points here are: 1. He was at the end of his resources (money) 2. Many bills remained unpaid (he didn't have enough money to pay them) 3. His co-workers quarreled and left with tools (this is a problem, but it's a result of the financial issues perhaps) 4. For one year no work was done (this is the consequence we're looking to explain) The question asks for the main reason he failed to continue in 1828. The options are: A. Poor health (he had a health breakdown earlier, but the passage doesn't say he was in poor health in 1828 when he came back) B. Ran out of money (he was at the end of his resources) C. Co-workers argued and left (this happened, but it's listed as a consequence of being out of money, not the main cause) D. Spent all money on bills (the passage says bills remained unpaid, not that he spent all on them) The main
